title: Chittoorkavu Devi Temple
tagline: Chittoorkavu Devi Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Chittur-Thathamangalam is a town and municipality in the Palakkad district of Kerala State, India.
overview.description: Chittur-Thathamangalam is a town and municipality in the Palakkad district of Kerala State, India. It is the headquarters of Chittur taluk, 13 km (8.1 mi) southeast of Palakkad, on the banks of the Kannadipuzha, a major headstream of the Bharathapuzha, the second-longest river in Kerala. A mini civil station is also located here, which provides government-related services. It consists of two areas, Chittur and Thathamangalam, which together form the municipality.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Chittur taluk
topPlaces.description: Chittur is one among the 6 taluks in Palakkad district of Indian state of Kerala. There are 30 revenue villages in chittur taluk.

topPlaces.name: Chittur, Palakkad
topPlaces.description: Chittur is a village in Palakkad district in the state of Kerala, India. A part of it is governed by the Nalleppilly gram panchayat, along with the villages of Nalleppilly and Thekkedesom.

topPlaces.name: Polpully
topPlaces.description: Polpully is a village and gram panchayat in Palakkad district in the state of Kerala, India. The village is located 10 km (6.2 mi) from Palakkad city, and 5 km (3.1 mi) from Chittur.

topPlaces.name: Pudussery Central
topPlaces.description: Pudussery Central is a census town in Palakkad district in the state of Kerala, India. Pudussery Central comes under the administration of the Pudusseri gram panchayat.

topPlaces.name: Pudussery East
topPlaces.description: Pudussery East is a village in the Palakkad district, Kerala, India. Pudussery Central, Pudussery East, and Pudussery West fall under the administration of the Pudusseri Gram Panchayat.

topPlaces.name: Ootukulangara Bhagavathy Temple, Peruvemba
topPlaces.description: Oottukulangara Bhagavathy temple is the Hindu temple situated in Peruvemba village of Palakkad district in the state of Kerala. The primary deity of the temple is Sri Ootukulangara Bhagavathy along with Lord Ganapathy and Lord Ayyappan.

topPlaces.name: Peruvemba
topPlaces.description: Peruvemba is a village and gram panchayat near Chittur-Thathamangalam in the Palakkad district of Kerala, India. Within the village is the Ootukulangara Bhagavathy Temple.

topPlaces.name: Kodumbu Subramanya Swamy Temple
topPlaces.description: Kodumbu Subramaniya Swamy Temple is one of the oldest Murugan temples in Kerala region. It is located in the village of Kodumba in Palakkad district, 10 kilometres (6.2 mi) southeast of Palakkad and southwest of Coimbatore in the valley of the Soka Nasini river, Kerala, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Chittoorkavu Devi Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Chittoorkavu Devi Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Chittoorkavu Devi Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Coimbatore International Airport (49 km) and the nearest railway station is Coimbatore Junction (41 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Chittoorkavu Devi Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Chittoorkavu Devi Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Chittoorkavu Devi Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Chittoorkavu Devi Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Chittoorkavu Devi Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Chittur taluk, Chittur, Palakkad, Polpully, Pudussery Central, Pudussery East.
